content.full_text:
T

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 27, 1998

Kirk Alan Pessner
Law Office of
Russell H. Miller
20 Park Road, Suite E
Burlingame, CA 94010-4443

Re: Your Request for Assistance
Our File No. I-98-099

Dear Mr. Pessner:

This is in response to your request for advice under the campaign provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Since you are asking for the definition of a phrase used in the
Act and not requesting advice on behalf of an identified client, your letter is considered a request
for informal assistance.²

QUESTIONS AND CONCLUSIONS

1. Does the term "guaranteed overnight delivery service" as used in Sections 81007 and
81007.5 refer to the delivery company (e.g. Federal Express, UPS, etc.) or to the requirement to
send the report "guaranteed overnight?"

The term "guaranteed overnight delivery service" refers to the method of
service, not a specific commercial courier. Federal Express, UPS, and other
delivery companies may provide a delivery service which guarantees that your
parcel or letter arrives at its destination the following day. If a statement is
required to be filed by personal delivery or guaranteed overnight delivery, the
statements must be sent through a courier which can guarantee next day delivery.
(Bagatelos Advice Letter, No. A-86-305.)

¹Government Code Section 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18100-18995,
of the California Code of Regulations.

²Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18329(c)(3).)

File No. I-98-099
Page 2

2. Do Sections 81007 and 81007.5 permit the filing of a report (other than the 12-day
pre-election report) with a "guaranteed overnight delivery service," but request that the report be
filed using two-day "guaranteed" service?

No. Sections 81007 and 81007.5 refer to a delivery service which provides
guaranteed next day delivery i.e., overnight delivery service, as opposed to a service
which guarantees a service which is other than overnight delivery.

3. If a report is sent through a guaranteed delivery company but is filed with the
filing officer by the filing deadline, would it comply with Section 81007 and 81007.5 even if
the service requested is a two-day guaranteed delivery?

If a statement is received by the filing officer on or before the due date, the
method of delivery is not an issue. It only becomes an issue if the statement is
received after, but sent prior to, the deadline. When a report or statement or copies
thereof have been sent by first-class mail or by any other guaranteed overnight
delivery service to the filing officer, it shall for purposes of any deadline be deemed
to have been received on the date of the deposit in the mail or of receipt by that
delivery service. (Keck Advice Letter, No. I-92-527.) If the statement is sent by a
means other than first class mail, personal delivery or guaranteed overnight
delivery, the filing officer may not use the day the statement is received by the
courier as the date filed. A statement transmitted in a manner other than described
above would be considered filed on the day the statement is received by the filing
officer.

I trust this answers your questions. If you have any other questions, please feel free to
contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division

SGC/JET/jt
